behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires validating an external HTTP error contract (401) plus non-forwarding side effect, which matches SYSTEM TRIAD error-path/visible-effect checks. Integration with only function-level stubs can miss raw-body/signature handling differences at the real HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real HTTP webhook request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` against the exact raw payload; assert `401` is returned and verify via queue readback/inspection that no event was enqueued.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an externally visible ordering guarantee with a persistent boundary. Per ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE, proving write-before-ack needs real readback around the actual queue boundary, not only mocked call order.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real queue adapter, issue a webhook over HTTP and instrument timestamps/readback so the queue write is durably observable before the `200` response is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is mainly orchestration across local modules (handler, enqueue, async processor) and can be reproduced with an in-process slow processor stub. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require real external contract checks beyond return-before-finish timing, and no OS/network/subprocess-specific failure is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + a deliberately slow downstream worker in-process; assert the HTTP handler completes immediately after enqueue while worker completion happens later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock SLA under concurrency and requires real runtime scheduling/HTTP behavior. Mocked timing and purely in-process unit boundaries can hide event-loop/backpressure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service and send concurrent real HTTP requests while earlier jobs are intentionally slow; measure end-to-end wall-clock latency and assert each response is received within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module dedupe/state-handoff behavior (ID tracking + queue write path). Unit tests for a dedupe helper alone can give false confidence; wiring-level check is needed. Rejected System because no explicit real external contract/OS/network requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real dedupe store/repository wiring and queue adapter in-process; submit the same event ID twice and assert only one queue write occurs.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires verifying external HTTP error-path contract shape (status + header), which is SYSTEM TRIAD evidence. Stub-only checks can miss header/status behavior at the HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a real/full-capacity condition (or real adapter full signal) through the HTTP endpoint and assert response is `503` with correct `Retry-After` header semantics.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: The word 'actually' plus persistent queue capacity enforcement calls for validating real queue implementation behavior at its external boundary, including real rejection behavior. Rejected Integration because mocks/fakes can trivially enforce limits and miss real persistence/concurrency semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a real persistent queue with a small capacity, fill it to limit, then attempt one more enqueue and assert the real adapter rejects it with the expected failure contract.
